title: ruta chalepensis subsp. angustifolia
intro: Ruta chalepensis subsp. angustifolia, also known as Egyptian rue, is a plant species from the rutaceae family, native to the Middle East and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat conditions such as anxiety, insomnia, and inflammation due to its sedative, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Egyptian rue include reducing stress and promoting relaxation, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of menopause and menstrual cramps. Egyptian rue can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it can also be used topically to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne.
